rulings.Case Title: WALTER DODSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 19, 2008, line 1, DEFENDANT CUMMINS ENGINE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR PER OCTOBER 14, 2008 DISMISSAL. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: WALTER DODSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 19, 2008, line 1, DEFENDANT HENNESSY INDUSTRIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THE SEPARATE STATEMENT FAILS TO COMPLY WITH CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1350. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE BY RELIANCE ON RESPONSES TO GENERAL INTERROGATORY RESPONSES. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: WALTER COE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 19, 2008, line 2, DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. ASSUMING THE PABCO AGREEMENT IS ADMISSIBLE, DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE. THE MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION UNDER D'AMICO IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S RECOLLECTION WAS REFRESHED. PLAINTIFF PRESENTS EVIDENCE THAT CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. SINCE THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS BASED ONLY ON THE ISSUE OF EXPOSURE, IT IS DENIED AS WELL. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL BELL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Strike And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion To Strike Declarations Of Charlie Ay, Kenneth Cohen And Richard Cohen
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 19, 2008, line 7, DEFENDANT SEARIVER MARITIME, INC.'S Motion To Strike Declarations Of Charlie Ay, Kenneth Cohen And Richard Cohen. THE RULINGS WILL BE AVAILABLE AT THE HEARING. IN GENERAL, IT APPEARS THAT THE SUBSTANCE OF THEIR TESTIMONY IS ADMISSIBLE. =(302/PJM/VC

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL BELL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 19, 2008, line 7, DEFENDANT THE GOODYEAR TIRE & RUBBER COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE MOTION IS GRANTED. TAKEN TOGETHER, PLAINTIFF'S TESTIMONY AND RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES ARE FACTUALLY DEVOID FOR FAILURE TO PROVIDE ANY BASIS FOR THE CONTENTION THAT DEFENDANT'S PRODUCTS HANDLED BY PLAINTIFF CONTAINED ASBESTOS. (SEE RESPONSE TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY 8.) THE OBJECTIONS TO THE DE MARSE DECLARATION ARE SUSTAINED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT RAISES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL BELL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 19, 2008, line 7, DEFENDANT SEARIVER MARITIME, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE MEERE DECLARATION IS ADMITTED. THE DECLARATION OF KELLER IS NOT FOR LACK OF KNOWLEDGE AS TO EVENTS AT ISSUE THAT TOOK PLACE PRIOR TO 1977. THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE SHIFTED BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S FACTUALLY DEVOID DISCOVERY RELATED TO THE CONDITION OF THE SHIP AT TURNOVER. PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT ON THE ISSUE OF THE VESSEL BEING ON THE RIVER AND ON ISSUE OF TURNING THE VESSEL OVER TO THE STEVEDORE IN A REASONABLY SAFE CONDITION. THE DECLARATIONS OF AY AND COHEN ELABORATE ON THE RISK. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL BELL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 19, 2008, line 7, DEFENDANT DURAMETALLIC CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ADJUDICATION AS TO THE NEGLIGENCE AND STRICT LIABILITY CLAIMS IS DENIED. THE BURDEN SHIFTED AND PLAINTIFF PRESENTS EVIDENCE THAT RAISES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION THAT IS NOT BARRED BY D'AMICO. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO FALSE REPRESENTATION, INTENTIONAL TORTS AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL BELL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 19, 2008, line 7, DEFENDANT ITT CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE MOYER DECLARATION IS ADMISSIBLE. DEFENDANT DID NOT SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE FOR THE RECORD IS NOT SUFFICIENTLY CLEAR FOR THE COURT TO CONCLUDE THAT THE PRODUCTS IDENTIFIED BY PLAINTIFF AND MOYER ARE THE SAME PRODUCTS. SEE PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSE TO UNDISPUTED FACTS 17, 18 AND 19. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE CLAIM FOR FALSE REPRESENTATION AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD BOVEE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 19, 2008, line 8, DEFENDANT SLAKEY BROTHERS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE. PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AND IS NOT BARRED BY D'AMICO. DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS LIMITED TO PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AND PLAINTIFF HAS CREATED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT ON THAT ISSUE. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA CORRECTIONAL PEACE OFFICERS' et al VS. MATTHEW C KRAMER
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 19, 2008, line 9, DEFENDANT MATTHEW KRAMER'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION WHICH IS WITHIN THE EXCLUSIVE JURISDICTION OF OHSA. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE 2ND AND 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION WHICH ALLEGE COMMON LAW CLAIMS WHERE AN ALLEGED DUTY IS BREACHED. GIVEN THE RELIEF SOUGHT, PLAINTIFF DID NOT NEED TO FILE A TORT CLAIM - GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 814. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID G. SUADI,D.O. VS. BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion Determining Issuance Of Mandatory Injunction Staying The Termination Ofpetitioner'S Status As A Participating Provider Under The Insurance Issued By Respondent
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 19, 2008, line 16, Motion Determining Issuance Of Mandatory Injunction Staying The Termination Of petitioner's Status As A Participating Provider Under The Insurance Issued By Respondent IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE IMMEDIATE IRREPARABLE HARM OR THE RIGHT TO RELIEF UNDER POTVIN MET LIFE INSURANCE CO. (2000) 22 CAL.4TH 1060, 1071. IT ALSO DOES NOT APPEAR THAT PLAINTIFF WILL PREVAIL ON THE MERITS FOR THE BY-LAW IS CLEAR AND DEFENDANT WAS MERELY DOING WHAT THE PARTIES AGREED TO DO. =(302/PJM/JW)
